action: Add a commit-bound, read-only workflow that validates the corrected continuity delta and produces a reviewable noncanonical candidate index with retained evidence.
selection_basis: M-008 required clean-checkout validation and byte-reviewed candidate generation before any canonical update. The workflow is the highest-priority additive control that can reproduce those requirements without manually rewriting the index or claiming an unavailable completed run.
memory_recovered: A canonical memory merge is not a file replacement; it is a commit-bound evidence event whose input index, delta, candidate bytes, source identity, validation transcript, semantic checks, and retained predecessor must remain independently reviewable.
contradictions_resolved: Separated candidate generation from canonical publication by granting the workflow read-only repository permissions.
Separated committed workflow source from completed workflow-run evidence.
Separated deterministic merge readiness from truth of the indexed claims.
Preserved the invalid predecessor delta as retained history rather than deleting or silently normalizing it.

observed: workflow source binds exact checkout and base blob identities
workflow retains candidate, test, validation, and semantic-review outputs
inferred: the workflow should reproduce the committed merge contract in a clean GitHub-hosted checkout when dispatched
proposed: dispatch the workflow, inspect retained outputs, and publish the canonical index only through a separate reviewed commit
superseded: manual byte editing as the next integration step
treating source inspection as equivalent to workflow execution

privacy_boundary: Public repository files, commit and blob identifiers, schema vocabulary, and abstract continuity mechanics only. No raw private chat, health or pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, or unreviewed uploads were published.
next_action: Dispatch Continuity index merge verification at an immutable commit. Review the retained candidate SHA-256, appended CM-0013 through CM-0016, unchanged privacy boundary, merge history, and exact base binding. Only then commit the candidate bytes as operations/continuity/continuity-index.json in a separate reversible commit.
